qas.q:
_______________________________ is the maintenance mission. The maintenance function ensures assigned aircraft and equipment are _____________________ to meet mission needs
AFI 21-101, para 1.3
qas.a:
- Aircraft and equipment readiness
- safe, serviceable, and properly configured

qas.q:
_________________ identifies abort reason and assigns responsibility
21-101 Table 4.3

Occurs on the next sortie or attempted sortie after corrective action has been taken and the system or sub-system indicates the same malfunction when operated
AFI 21-101, para 3.4.1.61. MXG/CC Responsibilities

System or subsystem indicates same malfunction
Occurs on the second through fourth sortie or attempted sortie after corrective action is taken
AFI 21-101, para 3.4.1.61. MXG/CC Responsibilities:

Signed off only after thorough troubleshooting
AFI 21-101, para 3.4.1.61. MXG/CC Responsibilities:

Authorized to impound aircraft or equipment
Appoints impound official
AFI 21-101, Chap. 9

The MXG/CC and MXG/CD (or equivalents) can release aircraft and equipment
Can be delegated in writing
AFI 21-101, Chap. 9

Will be MSgt or above and is appointed by Impoundment Authority
Individual investigates mishaps / files report
AFI 21-101, Chap. 9
Impoundment reasons
qas.a:
-Ground or flight mishaps as defined in AFI 91-204
-Uncommanded flight maneuver
-Flight control malfunction
-Intentional damage, tampering, or sabotage
-Engine anomalies
-Inadvertent release or explosive mishap
-Physiological incidents
-In-flight fire

qas.q: What 5 CDDAR exercises will Mx Flight conduct on a periodic basis?

-Conduct crash recovery exercises at least annually

-Involve all wing agencies

-Simulate lifting of operational aircraft

-Attach and apply tension to lifting cables at least every three years

-Demonstrate crash recovery equipment

What are the Landing Status codes?
qas.a:
Code 0 Ground Abort
Code 1 Aircraft Mission capable with no additional discrepancies
Code 2 Aircraft or system has minor discrepancies assignment.
Code 3 Aircraft or system has major discrepancies
Code 4 Aircraft or system has suspected or known radiological, chemical, or biological contamination.
Code 5 Aircraft or system has suspected or known battle damage.

qas.q: Table 4.2. System Capability Code 0
qas.a: System flown with a known discrepancy, no additional discrepancies Noted. System can be used.

qas.q: Table 4.2. System Capability Code 1
qas.a: System used and performed satisfactorily. No maintenance required.

qas.q: Table 4.2. System Capability Code 2
qas.a: System used and performed satisfactorily. A minor malfunction exists, but system is capable of further mission assignment.

qas.q: Table 4.2. System Capability Code 3
qas.a: System performance was unsatisfactory. This system did not cause an abort.

qas.q: Table 4.2. System Capability Code 4
qas.a: System performance was unsatisfactory. This system caused or contributed to an abort.

qas.q: Table 4.2. System Capability Code 5
qas.a: System out of commission prior to takeoff.

qas.q: Table 4.2. System Capability Code 6
Table 4.2. System Capability Code 7
Table 4.2. System Capability Code 8
qas.a: Aircraft or system has suspected or known radiological/biological contamination

qas.q: What are the 3 most common Deviation Cause Codes?
qas.a:
-GAA Ground Abort, before engine start, maintenance
-GAB Ground Abort, after engine start, before taxi, maintenance
-GAC Ground Abort, after taxi, maintenance

qas.q: What sections make up Maintenance Flight?

-Refurbishment Section (optional)
- Transient Aircraft (TA)
